description: Strengthen the MCP server's self-improvement bootstrapping loop so that AI agents automatically invoke the right prompts before tasks and contribute learnings back after completion — without requiring human prompting. Currently the instructions mention prompts and contribution, but the guidance is too passive and buried. After these changes, an AI agent connected to OpenTabs will: (1) proactively invoke build_plugin before building a plugin, (2) proactively invoke contribute_learnings after completing any task, and (3) write higher-quality contributions because the prompts provide concrete examples of good vs bad contributions.

userStories.title: Make SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS more directive about prompt invocation and contribution
userStories.description: Rewrite the SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant in mcp-setup.ts to make prompt invocation and learning contribution structurally unavoidable rather than passively mentioned. Move the contribution mandate to a more prominent position, strengthen the workflow trigger language, and add a clear 'before/after every task' framing.
acceptanceCriteria: The Contributing Learnings section appears BEFORE the Resources section in SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS
The workflow triggers section uses imperative language: 'You MUST invoke' not 'invoke the corresponding prompt'
The contribute_learnings trigger row says 'MANDATORY after completing ANY task' not '(always do this)'
SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS remains under 120 lines / 6000 chars (per the audit prompt's budget)
The instructions explicitly state: prompts contain accumulated AI learnings from previous sessions, not generic documentation
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant, lines 281-397)

Context:
The SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant is sent to every MCP client during the initialize handshake. It is the primary mechanism for telling AI agents how to use OpenTabs. Currently, the 'Contributing Learnings' section is at the very bottom (after Resources), making it easy to overlook. The workflow triggers table uses passive language ('invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding') that agents don't always follow.

Changes:
1. Move the 'Contributing Learnings (Mandatory)' section to immediately after 'Error Handling' and before 'Workflow Triggers' — this positions it as a core operational requirement, not an afterthought.
2. In the workflow triggers intro paragraph, change 'invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding' to 'you MUST invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding — do not attempt the task from general knowledge. These prompts contain accumulated patterns, gotchas, and step-by-step workflows from prior AI sessions that are critical for success.'
3. In the contribute_learnings row, change 'After completing any task (always do this)' to 'MANDATORY after completing ANY task'
4. Add a sentence to the Contributing Learnings section: 'The prompts and resources you receive were written and refined by previous AI sessions. You are reading successful patterns because prior sessions contributed them. Failing to contribute your own learnings breaks this cycle and degrades future sessions.'
5. Keep the overall size under 120 lines / 6000 chars by tightening other prose.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the security rules section — it is critical and correct.
- Do NOT remove any existing workflow trigger rows.
- Do NOT change the tool categories section.
- Preserve the exact same sections; just reorder and strengthen language.
- The Resources section listing resource URIs must remain (agents need it for fetching).

userStories.title: Improve the contribute_learnings prompt with concrete examples and structured output
userStories.description: Rewrite the contribute_learnings prompt text to include concrete before/after examples of good vs bad contributions, a checklist for identifying learnings from the session, and guidance on writing contributions that are immediately useful to the next AI session.
acceptanceCriteria: The prompt includes at least 2 concrete 'good contribution' examples showing actual text to add to a resource file
The prompt includes at least 2 'bad contribution' examples showing what NOT to write (too vague, too app-specific)
The prompt includes a session review checklist that references checking tool call results, error messages encountered, and API patterns discovered
The prompt explicitly tells the agent to check if contribute_learnings was called during this session and if not, do it now
Step 1 (Identify What You Learned) includes 'Check your tool call history in this session' as the first bullet
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/contribute-learnings.ts (the entire file, 73 lines)

Context:
The contribute_learnings prompt is invoked after completing a task. Currently it lists abstract categories of learnings (patterns, gotchas, resolution steps) but doesn't show concrete examples. AI agents often produce vague contributions like 'Added error handling pattern' instead of specific, useful entries like a new gotcha in the plugin-development resource.

Changes:
1. After Step 1's existing bullets, add a new subsection '### Session Review Checklist' with:
   - Check your tool call history for failed calls and how you fixed them
   - Check for API endpoints you discovered through network capture
   - Check for auth patterns that worked (or didn't)
   - Check for Zod schema patterns you used
   - Check for error messages you encountered and resolved

2. After Step 2, add a new '### Contribution Examples' section:
   Good: `## Common Gotchas\n\n19. **API returns HTML on 500 errors** — wrap response.json() in try-catch and parse the text body for error details when JSON parsing fails.`
   Good: `### Cookie-Based Auth with CSRF\n\nSome apps store the CSRF cookie name in window.__initialData.csrfCookieName — check bootstrap globals before hardcoding cookie names.`
   Bad: 'Added better error handling' — too vague, no actionable information
   Bad: 'Notion uses space IDs in the format xxx-xxx' — too app-specific, not reusable

3. In Step 3, add: 'When adding a gotcha, number it sequentially after the last existing entry. When adding a pattern, add it to the relevant subsection with a code example.'

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the embedded resource URIs (self-improvement, plugin-development, troubleshooting).
- Keep the same 5-step structure.
- The task parameter handling at the top must remain unchanged.

userStories.title: Strengthen the self-improvement resource with bootstrapping motivation and contribution workflow clarity
userStories.description: Update the self-improvement resource to better explain the bootstrapping value proposition — why agents should care about contributing — and add clearer guidance on the contribution workflow including what triggers it and how to verify contributions work.
acceptanceCriteria: The resource includes a 'Why You Should Care' section explaining that the agent's current success is due to previous sessions' contributions
The 'Contribution Cycle' section explicitly mentions that the build_plugin prompt's Phase 7 triggers contribution
The 'How to Trigger the Contribution Workflow' section mentions both explicit invocation AND the automatic trigger from build_plugin Phase 7
The resource includes a 'Verifying Your Contribution Works' subsection explaining that after editing and rebuilding, the next session will receive the updated content
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/resources/self-improvement.ts (the entire file, 76 lines)

Context:
This resource is served at `opentabs://guide/self-improvement` and is embedded into the contribute_learnings prompt. It explains the file-to-content mapping and contribution rules but is very mechanical — it doesn't motivate the agent to actually contribute.

Changes:
1. Add a new section '## Why You Should Care' after '## How It Works', containing:
   'Every piece of guidance you received in this session — the build_plugin workflow, the gotcha list, the auth extraction patterns — was written by a previous AI session that completed a similar task. If that session had not contributed back, you would be starting from scratch. Your contributions have the same impact: the next AI session that builds a plugin will benefit from patterns you discovered today. Failing to contribute means the next session will encounter the same issues you just solved, without your solution.'

2. In the 'Contribution Cycle' section, between step 2 and 3, add:
   '2.5. **AI invokes contribute_learnings** — either explicitly or via the mandatory Phase 7 of the build_plugin workflow.'

3. Expand 'How to Trigger the Contribution Workflow' to mention:
   - The build_plugin prompt's Phase 7 (Write Learnings Back) explicitly triggers this
   - The SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS mandate contribution after every task
   - The agent can also proactively contribute at any time

4. Add '## Verifying Your Contribution Works' section:
   'After editing a source file and running `cd platform/mcp-server && npm run build`, the compiled content updates immediately. Any new MCP session that connects will receive your updated resources and prompts. You can verify by calling `resources/read` with the resource URI — it will return your updated content.'

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the file-to-content mapping table — it is correct and complete.
- Do NOT change the 'Rules for Contributing' section.
- Keep the existing structure; add new sections in logical positions.

userStories.title: Update build_plugin prompt Phase 7 to be more explicit about what to contribute
userStories.description: Strengthen the Phase 7 (Write Learnings Back) section of the build_plugin prompt to be more explicit about what specific learnings from the plugin build should be contributed back, and to include a concrete checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 7 includes a checklist of specific things to check: auth pattern used, API discovery method, error handling patterns, Zod schema tricks, CORS workarounds, any new gotcha encountered
Phase 7 explicitly states 'If you encountered an error that is not in the Common Gotchas list, ADD IT'
Phase 7 mentions the specific files to check: build-plugin.ts (for workflow improvements), plugin-development.ts (for patterns/gotchas), troubleshooting.ts (for error resolutions)
Phase 7 remains the final phase (not reordered)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/prompts/build-plugin.ts (Phase 7, approximately lines 431-437)

Context:
Phase 7 currently says 'Every plugin build surfaces new patterns, gotchas, and techniques. You must write them back so the next AI session benefits automatically.' followed by a directive to invoke contribute_learnings. This is good but too abstract — the agent doesn't know WHAT specifically to contribute from its plugin build session.

Changes:
Replace the Phase 7 content (lines 431-437, the last paragraph of the template string) with an expanded version that includes:

1. Keep the existing intro paragraph about writing learnings back.
2. Add a checklist:
   - Did you discover a new auth extraction pattern? → Add to `plugin-development.ts` under 'Auth Token Extraction' or 'Advanced Auth Patterns'
   - Did you encounter an error not in Common Gotchas? → Add to the numbered gotcha list in `build-plugin.ts`
   - Did you find a new API discovery technique? → Add to Phase 2 of this prompt
   - Did you work around a CORS issue? → Add to troubleshooting resource
   - Did you use a Zod schema pattern worth documenting? → Add to `plugin-development.ts` under 'Zod Schema Rules'
3. Keep the directive to invoke contribute_learnings.
4. Keep the closing sentence about the self-improving loop.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other phase (1-6).
- Do NOT change the Common Gotchas list (that's separate).
- Do NOT change the prerequisites or core principle sections.
- Phase 7 must remain the final section in the prompt.

userStories.title: Update tests to reflect strengthened instructions and prompt content
userStories.description: Update the mcp-prompts.test.ts and mcp-setup.test.ts tests to verify the strengthened content: the reordered SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS sections, the new contribution examples in contribute_learnings, and the expanded Phase 7 in build_plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-prompts.test.ts verifies contribute_learnings prompt includes 'Session Review Checklist'
mcp-prompts.test.ts verifies contribute_learnings prompt includes 'Contribution Examples'
mcp-prompts.test.ts verifies build_plugin Phase 7 includes a contribution checklist
mcp-setup.test.ts handler count test still passes (7 handlers)
All existing tests continue to pass without modification (unless they assert on exact strings that changed)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-prompts.test.ts (contribute_learnings and build_plugin test sections)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts (may need minor updates if any test asserts on exact instruction text)

Context:
The existing tests verify prompt content through substring checks. After the changes in US-001 through US-004, some assertions may need updating and new assertions should be added.

Changes:
1. In `describe('resolvePrompt — contribute_learnings')`, add:
   - `test('includes session review checklist', () => { ... expect(text).toContain('Session Review Checklist'); })` 
   - `test('includes contribution examples', () => { ... expect(text).toContain('Contribution Examples'); })`

2. In `describe('resolvePrompt — build_plugin')`, add:
   - `test('Phase 7 includes contribution checklist', () => { ... expect(text).toContain('auth extraction pattern'); })`

3. Review all existing tests in mcp-setup.test.ts — the handler count test (line 1073) should still pass as we're not adding/removing handlers.

4. If any existing tests assert on exact text from SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS, update them to match the reworded content.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove any existing test.
- Do NOT change test infrastructure (createMockServer, mockExtra, etc.).
- New tests should follow the existing pattern of using `firstMessageText()` and `embeddedResourceUris()` helpers.
- Run `npm run test` to verify all tests pass.
